title: Bridge Essay: Bridging the schism of schizophrenia through yoga-Review of putative mechanisms.
concept: Intersubjectivity — embodied empathy, social predictive processing, second-person neuroscience.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Social Incarnation** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Social Incarnation — intersubjectivity, embodied empathy, social predictive processing, second-person neuroscience.
**Phase context:** Intersubjectivity — embodied empathy, social predictive processing, second-person neuroscience.

**Bridging the schism of schizophrenia through yoga-Review of putative mechanisms.** — Int Rev Psychiatry (2016). DOI: 10.1080/09540261.2016.1176905. PMID: 27187680. Schizophrenia patients experience a 'disconnect' at multiple levels-neuronal networks, mental processes, and interpersonal relationships. The resultant poor quality-of-life and functional disability are related to the persistent cognitive deficits and negative symptoms, which are rather resistant to conventional antipsychotic medications.
